items.title: Ebola outbreak may be spreading faster than first thought, WHO doctor warns
items.summary: Hundreds of cases are suspected in central Africa but experts fear the actual number may be much higher.

items.title: Xi told Trump that Putin might ‘regret’ invasion of Ukraine
items.summary: US president also suggested they should co-operate with Russian leader against the International Criminal Court

items.title: German government launches Uniper privatisation four years after rescue
items.summary: Berlin to reduce stake in gas importer from 99% to 25% following nationalisation triggered by Ukraine war

items.title: Nasdaq 100 Falls on Chipmaker Rout as Yields Rise
items.summary:
Wall Street’s crowded chipmaker trade continued to unravel, dragging down stocks on speculation that a surge from this year’s lows has gone too far amid war-fueled inflation risks that have sank bonds.
Omar Aguilar, CEO and CIO at Schwab Asset Management, discusses his outlook on the markets as 30-year yields climb to a level last seen in 2007. (Source: Bloomberg)

Terror in Europe : Lithuania to consider deploying NATO nuclear weapons targeting Russia and Kaliningrad
Submit your questions: Is Trump’s economic agenda working?
items.summary: Take part in a live Ask an Expert Q&A with the FT’s senior US columnists Edward Luce and Rana Foroohar on Thursday May 21 at 1pm BST

items.title: US Stocks Extend Declines as Yields Climb; Software Shares Gain
items.summary: US stocks opened lower on Tuesday, weighed down as US long-term yields touched their highest since 2007. Software shares rallied while chip stocks extended declines, signaling some rotation within the tech sector.

items.title: Distressed-Debt Deals Often End in ‘Hard Default,’ Moody’s Warns
items.summary: About a quarter of companies that go through a distressed exchange event end up in so-called “hard defaults” such as bankruptcy or a missed payment, according to a Moody’s Analytics report.

items.title: Shell Wins Investor Backing for Higher Executive Bonuses
items.summary: Shell Plc shareholders approved a new policy that could award larger bonuses to executive directors, moving the company closer to compensation levels at its US rivals.

items.summary:
Concluding a very fruitful visit to Norway. Held productive talks with Prime Minister Jonas Gahr Støre and participated in the India-Nordic Summit, where important decisions were taken to deepen cooperation in many areas.

I thank the people of Norway, PM Støre and the Government of Norway for their warmth and friendship.

@jonasgahrstore

In conflict, civilians always pay the highest price.

Yet even wars have rules.

Civilians must be protected — at all times, in all places.

They are #NotATarget.
